reason: Re-check lint + test bar — `dart format .` / `flutter analyze` / `flutter test` all green; widget tests under test/features/long_stitch/** cover AC-1 (wide-screen StitchVerticalImageList present + StitchImageStrip absent), AC-2 (row form + reorder), AC-4 (empty-state _EmptyHint visible with two CTAs), AC-5 (narrow-screen unchanged), AC-6 (controller method parity), AC-7 (independent scroll regions, no overflow). No existing test regressed.

Re-check widget structure on every touched widget — StitchVerticalImageList (Header Row + Expanded(SingleChildScrollView(ReorderableColumn)) or empty-state branch), _StitchVerticalImageRow (thumbnail + size text + × IconButton + drag_indicator Icon), and the edited stitch_editor_screen.dart (LayoutBuilder + SizedBox(panelWidth, Column(Expanded(list), Expanded(SingleChildScrollView(panel))))). const constructors preserved, no unnecessary Container, prop ordering follows existing patterns, a11y semantics for × button and drag handle present.

Re-check the responsive behavior table row for stitch_editor reflects the new structure (expanded / large = side panel split 50/50 between vertical list + controls panel, both with internal SingleChildScrollView). The compact / medium row is unchanged. Confirm no magic-number width comparisons were introduced — every branch still reads from windowSizeClassOf(context). Side panel width still uses LayoutBuilder + clamp(380, constraints.maxWidth * 0.25, 480).

Re-check Riverpod usage — StitchVerticalImageList subscribes via ref.watch(stitchEditorControllerProvider) once per build, reads notifier (ref.read(...notifier)) only inside callbacks, and does not duplicate state already owned by the controller. The shared _confirmStitchClear helper must take a BuildContext (for the dialog) without leaking provider access — clear() dispatch happens at the call-site, not inside the helper, to keep the helper UI-only.

Re-check that the screen's WindowSizeClass dispatch remains exhaustive — `useSidePanel = sizeClass == expanded || sizeClass == large` still covers all four buckets when grouped with the else branch. No new enum-typed branches introduced; if any future StitchMode dispatch survives in touched files (it should not — this task is layout-only), it still handles vertical + horizontal exhaustively.
